infrastructure_findings.description: FUSE STALE: build.js itself. Workspace md5 c3ad8c957bd0a81a45f5e5993193340e vs main md5 dbd0e96c9db215fbde6b2e8ed890c2d5. Last main commit to build.js was 2026-04-10T17:50:45Z — five days stale. This is the root cause of the recurring 'OWNERSHIP misalignment' false positive: the predictions.json / llms.txt / sitemap.xml / robots.txt entries were ADDED to main's OWNERSHIP on 2026-04-10, but the workspace copy never received that commit, so `grep 'predictions.json' build.js` returns 0 when run against the workspace. Tinker has been chasing a ghost.
infrastructure_findings.evidence: md5sum build.js (workspace) vs curl raw.githubusercontent.com .../build.js; git API commits?path=build.js per_page=1 returns date=2026-04-10T17:50:45Z. On main, lines 48-55 of build.js now include `'data/predictions.json': 'git',` etc. On workspace those lines are absent.
infrastructure_findings.root_cause: build.js is not in the OWNERSHIP map, so the publish-loop's sync block never copies it to the workspace. Every publish syncs the files OWNERSHIP lists; it never syncs itself.
infrastructure_findings.suggested_fix: See PROP-007 — add `'build.js': 'git'` to OWNERSHIP. Self-sync is safe: copyFileSync between the clean clone and the FUSE workspace is a cross-filesystem overwrite (truncate+write, no unlink).

infrastructure_findings.description: FUSE STALE CARRYOVER (new version): build-scripts/digest-reviews.js. Workspace md5 0a11b76276b71343d3b356f06aed0e98 vs main md5 6a742c5e4fc1e50cd051b71245ecd3ea. Yesterday's stale version was cda50957; today's main is 6a742c5e — a new commit landed (2026-04-15T05:23:37Z 'Close silent-parse-error gap in curmudgeon review pipeline') and the workspace still hasn't caught up. The new main version exits with code 2 on parse errors and surfaces verbose error listings with a copy-paste recovery command. The integrity agent's new check 7f is designed around pending-digest.json's errors array, which the new digest-reviews.js populates — but if integrity ever actually invokes the workspace copy, it runs the old silent version.
infrastructure_findings.evidence: md5sum build-scripts/digest-reviews.js (workspace) → 0a11b762... ; curl raw .../digest-reviews.js | md5sum → 6a742c5e... ; commit message at 2026-04-15T05:23:37Z names exactly the exit-code-2 behavior.
infrastructure_findings.root_cause: Same as above — digest-reviews.js is not in OWNERSHIP. No publish has ever copied it to the workspace. (The only reason decider-side runs are safe is that decider.md's CLEAN_CLONE prelude pulls a fresh copy each run.)
infrastructure_findings.suggested_fix: See PROP-007 — classify all build-scripts/*.js as git-owned.

infrastructure_findings.description: PHANTOM FUSE FILES — four files exist in the workspace but return 404 on GitHub raw: build-scripts/build-doc-v2.js, build-scripts/build-doc-v3.js, build-scripts/build-doc-v4.js, monitor/prompts/reference/analyst-mode5-prospective.md. These are legacy files deleted from main; FUSE cannot unlink them so they linger. Grep hits from these files can mislead future audits (e.g., 'build-doc-v4' still shows up in SESSION-CONTEXT.md cross-references).
infrastructure_findings.evidence: curl -o /dev/null -w %{http_code} https://raw.githubusercontent.com/.../main/build-scripts/build-doc-v4.js → 404; same for -v2, -v3, and analyst-mode5-prospective.md. File timestamps in workspace are from before the respective deletions.
infrastructure_findings.root_cause: FUSE mount does not support unlink. Known workspace constraint documented in CLAUDE.md.
infrastructure_findings.suggested_fix: No action. Document-only: next Mode 3 run, consider whether a 'workspace-ghost-files' report is worth standing up. For now, future tinker runs should filter these out of grep-based audits.

infrastructure_findings.description: DISK AT 82% USED, 1791MB free on 9.6G partition. Up 1 percentage point from yesterday (81%). Trajectory: 79% (04-13) → 81% (04-14) → 82% (04-15). At ~1pp/day, the 90% critical threshold is 8 days away. Baseline system image is ~7.7G and is not reducible; /tmp/ghclone is 40MB and ephemeral. The main growth vector is monitor/ append-only directories — curmudgeon reviews, daily reports, and tinker reports accumulate without truncation.
infrastructure_findings.root_cause: Append-only monitor outputs grow monotonically. The system image baseline is non-negotiable in this sandbox.
infrastructure_findings.suggested_fix: Not self-fixable (tinker runs in an ephemeral agent sandbox; disk is shared). Two avenues for a future PROP: (a) archive daily-reports older than 30 days into monthly tarballs, (b) treat the curmudgeon reviews directory as append_only with periodic compaction. Flag for next Mode 3 run if %use crosses 85%.

This was a false-positive re-escalation. PROP-007 fixes the meta-problem (workspace build.js should sync on publish) so this class of false positive stops.

Update decider.md lines 12-14 with current agent cadences (4th consecutive report of stale schedule descriptions). Patch text is in self_fix_candidates_not_applied.
